question:
Will the price of Bitcoin be above $116,000 on October 29?

Description: This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) on the date specified in the title has a final "Close" price higher than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance BTC/USDT, not according to other sources or spot markets.

Price precision is determined by the number of decimal places in the source.

evidences.title: Crypto Market Trends: Bitcoin Price Forecasts and the Rise of ...
evidences.content: ## Future Outlook ### Predictions for 2025 and Beyond The future outlook for Bitcoin and the cryptocurrency market remains optimistic, with several analysts predicting significant price appreciation. According to a report by Fool.com, the price of Bitcoin could reach nearly $150,000 by 2025 in a base case scenario, driven by continued institutional investment and the approval of Bitcoin ETFs. In a bull case scenario, the price could potentially reach $1 million or more. Bernstein’s report predicts that Bitcoin will reach $200,000 in 2025, $500,000 by 2029, and $1 million by 2033, driven by further institutional adoption and ETF inflows. ### Factors Influencing Future Trends Several factors will influence future trends in the cryptocurrency market:

evidences.title: Institutional Bitcoin Investment: 2025 Sentiment, Trends, and Market Impact
evidences.content: ### Analyst Price Targets and Projections With institutional adoption accelerating, analyst price targets for Bitcoin in 2025 have grown more ambitious—and more data-driven. By Q2, a consensus among leading analysts places Bitcoin’s potential in the $200,000 to $210,000 range within the next 12 to 18 months. These projections are grounded in models that account for stock-to-flow dynamics, network growth, and comparisons to gold’s market cap, as well as the continued influx of institutional capital via ETFs and other channels. [...] ### BlackRock’s iShares: Dominating the ETF Landscape BlackRock’s iShares Bitcoin Trust (IBIT) has quickly established itself as the heavyweight in the Spot Bitcoin ETF arena. By the end of Q1 2025, IBIT alone had attracted over $18 billion in AUM, thanks to BlackRock’s trusted brand, expansive distribution, and competitive fees. This dominance signals more than product success—it marks a watershed moment of institutional validation for Bitcoin. evidences.title: Bitcoin's Price Prediction For 2025: What To Watch in October - Forbes
evidences.content: ### Bullish Projections Bullish predictions for bitcoin's price in 2025 from posts on X suggest a significant upward trend. CNBC analyst Tom Lee’s target number is $250,000. Matthew Sigel of VanEck forecasts $180,000. These forecasts reflect a strong belief in continued institutional adoption, the impact of bitcoin ETFs, and the cyclical nature of bitcoin's price movements post-halving. Of course, mega-bulls like Cathie Wood and Michael Saylor expect multi-million dollar bitcoin in the next few years, though not necessarily in 2025. ### Bearish Projections [...] Institutional adoption was another major milestone. Leading firms like BlackRock and BNY Mellon embraced bitcoin, offering custody and trading solutions to their clients. This institutional confidence supported a dramatic and sustained rise in bitcoin’s price in 2024. Now, we may see a virtuous cycle of increased inflows and reduced volatility. Bitcoin’s position on corporate balance sheets also looks set to grow, with corporations like MicroStrategy expanding their holdings to record levels and dozens of other companies following suit. With regulatory frameworks becoming clearer and adoption growing across industries, b...

evidences.title: Bitcoin ETF Analysis Reveals Stunning Shift
evidences.content: A fundamental transformation in Bitcoin’s market structure is now undeniable. Recent analysis confirms a stunning shift: Exchange-Traded Funds (ETFs) have decisively become the primary driver of BTC price movements, marking a pivotal evolution from retail-driven on-chain activity to institutional capital flows. This development, reported in March 2025, fundamentally alters how analysts and investors understand cryptocurrency valuation. Bitcoin ETF Analysis Reveals a New Market Paradigm [...] Pre-ETF Primary Drivers (Pre-2024) Current Primary Driver (2025) On-chain transaction volume ETF net daily inflows/outflows Miner selling pressure Authorized Participant creation/redemption activity Exchange whale ratio Fund-level subscription data from issuers like BlackRock & Fidelity Retail sentiment on social media Institutional allocation reports and fund filings Expert Insight on the Institutional Landscape [...] Bitcoin ETF Analysis Reveals a New Market Paradigm For years, Bitcoin’s price discovery relied heavily on on-chain metrics like active addresses, transaction volume, and miner activity. However, comprehensive analysis now demonstrates that ETF fund inflows and outflows exert t...

evidences.title: Binance Listing Cost 2026: Fees, Requirements & Timeline
evidences.content: The exchange-side fee is commonly planned in the $300K–$800K worth of tokens range and is typically allocated toward marketing inside the exchange. The full listing price may also include liquidity and market making reserves, security deposits, audits, legal and compliance work, PR, community activity, and post-listing support. Beyond the exchange fee, teams should expect: – Liquidity and market‑making reserves: $50K–$150K depending on target pairs and depth. – Security deposits: from $100K . – Marketing and announcements: PR, KOLs, communities, partnerships. If you need support with centralized exchange listings, see our CEX listing support. ## What Shapes Binance Listing Fees? Binance runs a rigorous, data‑driven review. Fees and timelines are influenced by the following: [...] Clarifying terms helps planning: – Listing fee (marketing fee): the amount paid to Binance for marketing inside the exchange. – Listing price: your project’s total budget for the launch (includes fee in tokens, Market Making liquidity, security deposits). – Listing cost: a practical umbrella term covering both fee and supporting budgets. ## Binance Listing Price vs. Exchange Fee The price for listing on...

evidences.content: ## How Much Are Binance Fees? Binance offers a competitive, tiered fee structure for both spot and futures trading. For regular users, spot trading fees start at 0.1% for both maker and taker. Users can receive additional discounts by paying fees in BNB or increasing their trading volume to qualify for VIP levels. Binance also offers zero-fee trading on select BTC and FDUSD spot pairs. For the most accurate and up-to-date fee schedule, see the official Binance Fee Structure page. ## Is It Possible To Use Leverage or Margin Trading on Binance? evidences.title: Do Bitcoin ETFs Lead Price Discovery Following their Introduction in ...
evidences.content: Figure 3 shows the volatility estimates based on the Yang-Zhang range-based volatility estimates for the bitcoin spot and nine ETFs for the period from 11 January to 11 October 2024. Each tick marks the 12 months from January to December 2024. From the observation, the bitcoin spot and the GBTC appear to be the most volatile, as indicated by the highest peak around March 2024, when both instruments reached the highest volatility of about 0.09. In mid-July 2024, only bitcoin spot recorded the highest volatility of around 0.064. All bitcoin ETFs recorded lower volatility in the period mentioned. Although GBTC is not the most actively traded bitcoin ETF, it is the most volatile after the bitcoin spot. Fig. 3 [...] Table 2 shows the descriptive statistics of the daily closing prices and daily returns (rett = ln[pt/pt-1]) of bitcoin spot and all bitcoin ETFs studied for the nine-month period (11 January to 11 October 2024). DEFI does not appear to survive this period, as its closing prices on 11 October 2024 cannot be collected. BTCW is also excluded from the analysis as the ETF suffers from thin trading — its insufficient 5-min data has prevented us from proceeding with further stud...

evidences.title: Binance to Disclose Crypto Listing Fees, Donate 100% to Charity
evidences.content: ... CoinDesk # Binance to Disclose Crypto Listing Fees, Donate 100% to Charity Binance, the world's largest crypto exchange by trading volume, says all listing fees will now be declared and donated to its charity arm. · CoinDesk CoinDesk BTC-USD -1.79% BTCUSD=X ETH-USD -1.60% ETHUSD=X SNT-USD -2.74% Binance, currently the world's largest cryptocurrency exchange by trading volume, has announced that it will now disclose all cryptocurrency listing fees and donate the funds to charity. According to a company blog post published Monday, token projects seeking to be listed on the exchange will continue to be allowed to propose their own listing fees. The exchange will neither dictate such fees nor impose a minimum pricing level, the post indicates. [...] In August, a crypto project CEO claimed on Twitter that Binance had quoted 400 bitcoin in an email as a fee for listing the firm's asset in August. But Zhao later denied the claim, saying Binance never quoted fees via email. He went on to state at the time: "We don't list shitcoins even if they pay 400 or 4,000 BTC. ... Question is not 'how much does Binance charge to list?' but 'is my coin good enough?'" Binance image via Shuttersto...

evidences.title: Binance Fees 2025: Trading, Deposit & Withdrawal Costs - Coinspot.io
evidences.content: Crypto withdrawals operate differently. Network congestion and changing on-chain conditions can shift effective pricing, so the fee you see may vary over time. Each asset includes a minimum withdrawal and a fixed fee component. For Bitcoin, the minimum currently sits at 0.0004 BTC, and the flat network fee is 0.0002 BTC, independent of transfer size. If a preferred fiat option isn’t available for deposits or payouts, Binance P2P can be an alternative. It connects buyers and sellers directly, which can add payment-method flexibility. At present, Binance P2P supports eight coins, including USDT, USDC, BTC, and ETH, and offers 700+ settlement methods depending on location and counterparties. [...] ## Binance NFT Trading Fees The Binance NFT marketplace uses tiered fees similar to other products. For sellers, the platform fee starts at 0.9% and can decline with higher VIP levels, potentially reaching 0.1% at the top tiers. If you primarily hold blue-chip NFTs, a special holding tier may apply. Starting at 0.9%, the rate can move down to about 0.6% as your 30-day NFT valuation grows. ## Binance Fiat Trading Fees For fiat pairs, the tiered schedule starts at 0.1000% for makers and 0.1...

Copy `curl -g 'localhost:8000/replay?options={"exchange":"binance-futures","filters":[{"channel":"depth","symbols":["btcusdt"]}],"from":"2023-03-01","to":"2023-03-02"}'` Tardis-machine is a locally runnable server that exposes API allowing efficiently re...

Binance.US is the only major U.S. exchange to offer 0% maker fees and 0.01% taker fees for all new and existing customers, with no subscription fees or trading volume requirements. Trading fees are determined by your 30-day trading volume\, calculated on a rolling basis every day at 8 p.m. EDT. \Excludes self-trading and Tier 0 trading pairs Get a 5% discount on Advanced Trading (Spot Trading) fees when you pay with BNB. Check the table below to see how fees vary by tier. [...] Check the table below to see how fees vary by tier. In the Soft-Stake program, Binance.US deducts a 90% service fee from earned rewards in exchange for customer benefits. See our Terms and Staking FAQ for more info. evidences.title: BINANCE LIST COIN FEE $0? 🧐
evidences.content: I. Controversy about listing fees on Binance exchange In fact, there was previously a case where the CEO of Moonrock Capital revealed that if a project wants to list on Binance, they must 'contribute' 15% of the total supply of tokens to the exchange as a listing fee.😱😱 However, Yi He - Co-Founder of Binance clarified that since 2018, Binance has publicly stated that the listing fee is essentially a voluntary donation to the Binance Charity Fund, projects propose the amount, there is no minimum amount. After that, Andre Cronje - founder of Sonic Labs also shared that Binance supported them to list Sonic with a fee of 0 while Coinbase declared free listing but demanded 60 million to list Sonic. [...] - The ratio of airdrop to excessive fundraising - High FDV (over 1 billion dollars) => Prices will tend to drop sharply 🩸 For example: $BIO airdrop for launchpool nearly 70 million dollars, more than double the raised capital of 30 million dollars. 🔥 Not to mention the valuation reaching 2.3 billion dollars for the completely new DeSci sector with no application => The price of BIO has dropped 90% since listing!Image 7don't forget to support Tùng 🥳🥳🥳🥳 $BTC$ETH Image 8: ETH ETH 1,755....

evidences.title: CME Gearing Up to Launch Bitcoin Volatility Futures Independent ...
evidences.content: Those ETFs tracked CME's Bitcoin Volatility Index, or BVX, which, since 2024, has offered a real-time benchmark of the volatility of Bitcoin options contracts traded on CME. Put simply, the BVX tracks whether the market, at any given moment, is betting that Bitcoin's price will swing wildly or stay steady. CME's Bitcoin volatility futures would also track the BVX, which is published every second between 7 a.m. and 4 p.m. CT on the marketplace's trading days. [...] "With our new Bitcoin volatility futures, traders will be able to invest or hedge against the future volatility of Bitcoin, allowing them to access a critical new layer of risk management," Giovanni Vicioso, CME's global head of crypto products, said in a statement. Financial products tracking Bitcoin volatility have come in vogue this year, as institutions and retail traders seek increasingly sophisticated ways to hedge against long-term Bitcoin positions. In March, CoinShares filed to offer Wall Street's first Bitcoin volatility ETFs.

evidences.title: Cryptocurrency Market Dynamics Around Bitcoin Futures Expiration ...
evidences.content: BITO, the first of its kind, was incepted on 10/18/21. As the ProShares investment prospect points out, BITO invests in Bitcoin futures and does not invest in spot Bitcoin. There is no guarantee the fund will closely track Bitcoin returns. But it does sufficiently so, and for a long time, up until January 2024, it was the only trustworthy way for institutional investors that wanted BTC exposure in ETF form. Then came the first spot, U.S. ETFs (European and Canadian were before, but they were thinly traded and hence had a negligent influence on the price of spot Bitcoin), which is fast-forward and another story. [...] An astute reader would see that “price action” (returns around in the vicinity of expiration day) kind of flipped around the dates before and after the BITO launch. The daily pattern in the BTC returns in the period before the BITO introduction (2017-2021) is similar to the whole period (2017-2024). On the other hand, the pattern in the daily returns after the BITO ETF introduction (2021-2024) is reversed, especially on days preceding the expiration and on the expiration day itself. Finally, let’s just quickly look at the returns of BITO ETF (obviously, we track it...

evidences.title: A dive into liquidity demographics for crypto asset trading | S&P Global
evidences.content: Figure 17 shows the market depth on Binance for the asset pair EUR-USDT in euros for the 1% and 10% band (up and down) from the midpoint of the bid-ask price. Of note is the shallow market of less than €5 million for the 1% band and less than €8 million for the cumulative 10% band. This is not surprising as stablecoins have more utility on-chain than off-chain. By contrast, on Binance, USDT liquidity is much greater against other digital assets such as ETH and BTC than against fiat markets.Figure 18 shows a much higher market depth for BTC-USDT and ETH-USDT for the 1% band around the mid-price of USDT 15 million and USDT 30 million, respectively. [...] Figure 19 shows the average 1% market depth for selected markets (Binance) is lowest for EUR-USDC and highest for BTC-USDT. Crypto-USDT markets show a significantly higher market depth than crypto-fiat markets, which involve banks, fees, know your customer and other compliance measures. Crypto-USDT does not have any traditional banking constraints and is integrated seamlessly into trading strategies. External events or market news can affect market depth. During events of high volatility, such as the Silicon Valley Bank crisis in...

evidences.title: Within-Venue Monitoring of BTC/USDT Liquidity and Resiliency on ...
evidences.content: Keywords: Bitcoin; market microstructure; order flow; queueing theory; market quality; liquidity risk; digital assets; market resiliency Graphical Abstract ## 1. Introduction Binance spot BTC/USDT is a useful venue for studying short-horizon liquidity and resiliency because it combines continuous high-turnover trading with auditable trade prints and aggressor-side information. Those features support venue-level diagnostics built directly from transaction data and make it possible to track how trading activity, signed flow, and recovery conditions evolve through time inside a single market.
